enrichment.short_description: Free practice management software for health practitioners, covering scheduling, patient records, invoicing, and telehealth.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Halaxy offers free practice management software for health practitioners, including scheduling, patient records, invoicing, and telehealth tools.

features.title: Appointment scheduling
features.description: Calendar management, online bookings, automated reminders, and built-in telehealth for appointments.

features.title: Patient management
features.description: Stores patient records and health information, with an AI Scribe capability for clinical documentation.

features.title: Clinical invoicing and rebates
features.description: Generates clinical invoices and processes funding body rebates such as Medicare, DVA, and NDIS.

features.title: Financial reporting
features.description: Tracks practice revenue and generates financial reports for practitioners.

features.title: Clinical tools and templates
features.description: Provides over 700 clinical tools and templates for practitioners.

features.title: Patient portal
features.description: Gives patients access to a personal health record portal.

pros: Core practice management platform is free with no sign-up cost or credit card required
Unlimited appointments, practitioners, and locations on the free plan
Includes built-in telehealth and a patient health record portal
Over 700 clinical tools and templates included at no cost
cons: Add-on features like SMS reminders, rebates, e-scripts, and AI Scribe are billed per credit
Payment processing carries a 1.5%-1.9% transaction fee plus a flat fee
Advanced features like white-label branding and API access require a paid subscription starting at 25 credits/month

faqs.question: Is Halaxy free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes, the core practice management platform — including scheduling, patient records, invoicing, and reporting — is free with no credit card required.

faqs.question: How do Halaxy's add-on credits work?
faqs.answer: Optional features like SMS messaging, funding body rebates, e-scripts, and AI Scribe are billed on a pay-as-you-go credit basis, at roughly AUD $0.15-$0.22 per credit.

faqs.question: Does Halaxy support telehealth?
faqs.answer: Yes, telehealth is included as part of the appointment scheduling features.

faqs.question: Does Halaxy charge for payment processing?
faqs.answer: Yes, processing patient payments carries a fee of 1.5%-1.9% plus a flat fee per transaction, depending on tier.

use_cases.title: Solo practitioners
use_cases.description: Individual practitioners can run scheduling, records, and invoicing at no cost using the free core platform.

use_cases.title: Multi-practitioner clinics
use_cases.description: Clinics with several practitioners and locations can use unlimited practitioners/locations on the free tier, adding paid credits for SMS or rebates as needed.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Halaxy?
paragraphs: Halaxy is a practice management platform for health practitioners that combines appointment scheduling, patient records, clinical invoicing, and financial reporting in one free core system.

The platform includes over 700 clinical tools and templates, a patient health record portal, and built-in telehealth, with optional paid add-ons for SMS reminders, funding body rebates, e-scripts, and an AI Scribe feature.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Halaxy is for
paragraphs: Halaxy suits solo health practitioners and multi-practitioner clinics that want free core practice management software and are willing to pay per-credit for optional extras like SMS reminders, rebate processing, or AI-assisted documentation.

enrichment.short_description: HIPAA-compliant communication platform for healthcare teams, offering secure messaging, texting, video visits, and e-fax.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Spruce Health is a HIPAA-compliant platform for secure patient messaging, texting, video visits, and team communication, from $24/user/month.

features.title: Secure messaging
features.description: Share files and audio notes with patients through HIPAA-compliant secure messaging.

features.title: Two-way SMS texting
features.description: Send and receive patient texts with a dedicated medical phone number, plus AI voicemail transcription.

features.title: Video telehealth visits
features.description: Conduct video visits directly with patients from the same platform used for messaging.

features.title: E-fax
features.description: Send and receive faxes digitally without a physical fax machine.

features.title: Team messaging and tasks
features.description: Internal team chat with task assignment for staff collaboration.

features.title: VoIP phone system
features.description: Call routing, self-service call flows (IVR), and after-hours triage on the Communicator plan.

features.title: Digital payments
features.description: Collect patient payments through the platform.

pros: 14-day free trial with no credit card required
Effectively unlimited usage with no per-text, per-call, or per-fax charges
Available on web, iOS, and Android
HIPAA BAA included on both pricing tiers
cons: Per-user pricing means costs rise with team size, and all users must be on the same plan
SMS texting requires a one-time $19.50 registration fee
API access and integrations require the higher-priced Communicator plan

faqs.question: Does Spruce offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, a 14-day free trial is available with no credit card required, covering all Basic plan features except SMS texting.

faqs.question: How is Spruce priced?
faqs.answer: Per user per month: $24 for Basic and $49 for Communicator, billed month-to-month, with annual contracts available for larger practices.

faqs.question: Is there a limit on messages or calls?
faqs.answer: No, most practices can use texting, calling, and faxing in an effectively unlimited way without per-use charges.

faqs.question: What platforms does Spruce support?
faqs.answer: Spruce is available via web browser and native iOS and Android apps.

use_cases.title: Solo medical practices
use_cases.description: Independent providers can manage patient messaging, calls, and video visits from one HIPAA-compliant inbox.

use_cases.title: Team-based clinics
use_cases.description: Multi-provider practices can add call routing, after-hours triage, and integrations with the Communicator plan.

features: Medical phone, text, video, and fax

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Spruce Health?
paragraphs: Spruce Health is a HIPAA-compliant communication platform that lets healthcare teams handle secure messaging, two-way SMS, video visits, e-fax, and internal team chat from a single inbox.
It is priced per user per month across two plans — Basic and Communicator — with the higher tier adding call routing, after-hours triage, and integrations.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Spruce Health is for
paragraphs: Spruce Health fits solo physicians, mental health practitioners, and team-based clinics that need HIPAA-compliant patient communication across text, phone, fax, and video in one place.

enrichment.short_description: AI patient communication platform for medical practices, featuring an AI voice agent, secure texting, and EHR integrations.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: OhMD provides AI-powered patient communication for medical practices, including an AI voice agent, secure texting, and integration with 85+ EHR systems.

features.title: AI voice agent (Nia)
features.description: An AI agent automatically answers, routes, and handles patient phone calls.

features.title: Two-way secure texting
features.description: Send and receive SMS through a text-enabled landline number.

features.title: Call-to-text conversion
features.description: Converts inbound phone calls into text conversations.

features.title: Voicemail transcription
features.description: Transcribes voicemails automatically for staff review.

features.title: EHR integration
features.description: Connects with 85+ electronic health record systems.

features.title: Appointment scheduling automation
features.description: Automates scheduling as part of the Automate plan.

features.title: Broadcast messaging and smart routing
features.description: Sends broadcast messages and routes patient conversations to the right staff member.

pros: Used by 1,200+ medical practices, including major institutions like Dana-Farber and Memorial Sloan Kettering
AI voice agent (Nia) automates routine patient calls to reduce staff workload
Integrates with 85+ EHR systems
Includes a signed BAA and multi-factor authentication for HIPAA compliance
cons: Entry-level Communicate plan starts at $300/month, higher than some competitors
Calling usage is billed separately on the Automate plan
Enterprise tier requires custom, quote-based pricing

faqs.question: Does OhMD offer a free plan or trial?
faqs.answer: No free plan or free trial is mentioned on OhMD's pricing page.

faqs.question: What does the Communicate plan include?
faqs.answer: Two-way SMS, a text-enabled landline, voice/video calling, voicemail transcription, call-to-text, website chat, and team messaging, starting at $300/month.

faqs.question: What does the Automate plan add?
faqs.answer: Everything in Communicate plus the Nia AI voice agent for automated patient calls, EHR integration, and appointment scheduling, starting at $500/month, with calling usage billed separately.

faqs.question: Does OhMD integrate with EHR systems?
faqs.answer: Yes, OhMD integrates with more than 85 EHR systems.

use_cases.title: High call-volume small practices
use_cases.description: Practices needing two-way texting and call handling can start with the Communicate plan.

use_cases.title: Practices automating patient calls
use_cases.description: Busy practices can use the Automate plan's Nia AI voice agent to handle routine calls and scheduling automatically.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is OhMD?
paragraphs: OhMD is an AI-powered patient communication platform built for physician practices and multi-location healthcare networks, combining secure texting, call handling, and an AI voice agent called Nia.
Its Automate plan uses Nia to answer and route patient calls automatically, while integrating with more than 85 EHR systems for appointment scheduling and records access.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who OhMD is for
paragraphs: OhMD is aimed at physician practices and healthcare networks dealing with high call volumes that want to automate routine patient communication while staying HIPAA compliant.

name: Klara
skip_reason: klara.com no longer serves a standalone product site — it 302-redirects to ModMed's general site (modmed.com/what-we-do/patient-engagement/). Klara is now sold as a communication module within ModMed's EHR suite rather than an independent product, and the dedicated ModMed patient-engagement/patient-communication pages returned 404 or truncated/unreadable content on repeated fetch attempts. This leaves insufficient verifiable first-party facts (pricing, feature list, founding year, headquarters) to build an accurate, non-fabricated standalone enrichment.

enrichment.short_description: Operational AI platform automating patient access, engagement, intake, and payments for healthcare organizations.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Luma Health is an AI platform automating patient access, engagement, intake, and payments for health systems, specialty groups, and clinics.

features.title: Agentic Access
features.description: Automates order orchestration, referrals, fax processing, and waitlist management.

features.title: Agentic Engagement
features.description: Handles omnichannel messaging and conversational AI for two-way patient outreach.

features.title: Agentic Intake
features.description: Provides responsive digital forms, patient checklists, and clinic flow management.

features.title: Agentic Payments
features.description: Automates eligibility verification, prior authorization, and co-pay handling.

features.title: Spark
features.description: A healthcare-native AI core that coordinates the platform's automation capabilities.

features.title: No-code workflow builder
features.description: Lets healthcare teams build custom automated workflows without developer support.

features.title: EHR integrations
features.description: Connects with major EHRs including Epic, Oracle Health, MEDITECH, eClinicalWorks, Athenahealth, NextGen, Greenway, and Nextech.

pros: Trusted by 1,000+ healthcare organizations, including Banner Health and UAMS
Integrates with major EHRs including Epic, Oracle Health, and Athenahealth
No-code workflow builder allows customization without developers
Includes two-way AI translation for patient communication
cons: Pricing is not published and requires contacting sales
No free trial or free plan mentioned
Built primarily for larger healthcare organizations, which may be more complex than very small practices need

faqs.question: What does Luma Health's platform do?
faqs.answer: It automates patient access, engagement, intake, and payment workflows using an AI core called Spark.

faqs.question: Which EHR systems does Luma Health integrate with?
faqs.answer: It integrates with Epic, Oracle Health, MEDITECH, eClinicalWorks, Athenahealth, NextGen, Greenway, and Nextech.

faqs.question: Is Luma Health's pricing public?
faqs.answer: No, pricing is not disclosed on the website.

faqs.question: Does Luma Health support multiple languages?
faqs.answer: It includes a two-way AI translation feature for patient communication, though a specific language list is not published.

use_cases.title: Enterprise health systems
use_cases.description: Large health systems can automate referral orchestration, waitlist management, and patient intake at scale.

use_cases.title: Specialty and rural care providers
use_cases.description: Specialty groups and regional or rural providers can use no-code workflows and AI messaging to manage patient engagement with limited staff.
use_cases.audience: Specialty groups and rural/regional healthcare providers

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Luma Health?
paragraphs: Luma Health is an operational AI platform for healthcare organizations that automates patient access, engagement, intake, and payment workflows through a set of 'Agentic' modules powered by its Spark AI core.
It integrates with major EHR systems such as Epic, Oracle Health, and Athenahealth, and includes a no-code workflow builder for customizing automation.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Luma Health is for
paragraphs: Luma Health is built for enterprise health systems, specialty groups, and regional or rural care providers that want to automate patient-facing operations across access, intake, and payments.

enrichment.short_description: AI agent platform for healthcare patient communication, automating scheduling, intake, and multi-channel outreach.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Artera, formerly WELL Health, uses AI agents to automate patient scheduling, intake, and multi-channel communication for healthcare organizations.

features.title: AI voice agents
features.description: Automate scheduling, appointment management, and patient intake conversations.

features.title: Multi-channel communication
features.description: Reaches patients via voice, rich text, and email from one platform.

features.title: EHR integrations
features.description: Connects with major electronic health record systems.

features.title: AI Service Squads
features.description: Provide custom automation solution development for healthcare organizations.

features.title: Intelligent triage and warm handoff
features.description: Routes complex conversations from AI agents to human staff when needed.

features.title: 24/7 patient access
features.description: Supports after-hours care coordination and continuous patient access.

pros: Serves 1,000+ healthcare organizations with 2B+ patient interactions a year
Proprietary AI trained on 20B+ patient interactions without using PHI/PII for training
Supports 13+ medical specialties, including behavioral health, cardiology, and oncology
Offers 24/7 automated patient access and after-hours care coordination
cons: Pricing is not published and requires contacting sales
Company rebranded from WELL Health, which may create brand-recognition transition for existing customers

faqs.question: Is Artera the same company as WELL Health?
faqs.answer: Yes, Artera's site footer identifies it as '(Formerly WELL Health®)', indicating Artera is the rebranded successor to WELL Health.

faqs.question: What does Artera's platform do?
faqs.answer: It uses agentic AI voice, text, and email agents to automate patient scheduling, intake, and communication for healthcare organizations.

faqs.question: How many organizations use Artera?
faqs.answer: Artera states it serves 1,000+ healthcare organizations with over 2 billion patient interactions a year.

faqs.question: Does Artera train its AI on patient data?
faqs.answer: Artera states its AI does not use PHI/PII for model training.

use_cases.title: Federally Qualified Health Centers
use_cases.description: FQHCs can automate scheduling and intake communication with AI voice agents.

use_cases.title: Specialty practices and health systems
use_cases.description: Specialty practices across 13+ medical fields can use multi-channel AI communication for after-hours access and triage.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Artera?
paragraphs: Artera is an AI agent platform for healthcare patient communication, formerly operating as WELL Health, that automates scheduling, intake, and multi-channel outreach across voice, text, and email.
Its AI is trained on more than 20 billion patient interactions and includes intelligent triage that hands off complex conversations to human staff.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Artera is for
paragraphs: Artera is built for FQHCs, health systems, specialty practices, and federal agencies across 13+ medical specialties that want to automate patient scheduling and communication with AI agents.

enrichment.short_description: All-in-one communication and practice management platform with an AI receptionist for healthcare and small businesses.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Weave is an all-in-one communication platform with an AI receptionist, scheduling, payments, and review management for practices, from $199/month.

features.title: AI Receptionist
features.description: Handles inbound voice calls and text messages automatically.

features.title: Appointment scheduling and reminders
features.description: Automates online scheduling and patient reminders.

features.title: Payment processing
features.description: Supports text-to-pay and buy-now-pay-later options for patient payments.

features.title: Call Intelligence
features.description: Analyzes call sentiment for insight into patient interactions.

features.title: Review management
features.description: Includes an AI Response Assistant for managing and replying to customer reviews.

features.title: Digital forms and email marketing
features.description: Sends digital intake forms and email marketing campaigns.

features.title: Team chat and missed-call text automation
features.description: Provides internal team chat and automatically texts back missed calls.

pros: AI Receptionist automates inbound calls and texts to reduce staff workload
Headquartered in Lehi, Utah, with an established presence in the healthcare and dental market
Available via web browser, desktop app, and mobile app
Includes review management with AI-generated response suggestions
cons: Pricing for the Elite and Ultimate tiers is not published and requires contacting sales
Entry-level Pro plan starts at $199/month, which may be high for very small offices
No free plan or free trial mentioned

faqs.question: How much does Weave cost?
faqs.answer: The Pro plan starts at $199/month; pricing for the Elite and Ultimate plans requires contacting Weave directly.

faqs.question: What does the AI Receptionist do?
faqs.answer: It automatically answers inbound patient calls and texts, helping practices reduce missed calls and staff workload.

faqs.question: Where is Weave based?
faqs.answer: Weave is headquartered in Lehi, Utah.

faqs.question: What platforms is Weave available on?
faqs.answer: Weave is accessible via web browser, a desktop app, and mobile apps.

use_cases.title: Dental and medical practices
use_cases.description: Practices can automate patient reminders, payments, and reviews from one platform.

use_cases.title: Small businesses
use_cases.description: Small businesses outside healthcare can use Weave's communication and review tools to manage customer interactions.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Weave?
paragraphs: Weave is an all-in-one communication and practice management platform for healthcare practices and small businesses, combining an AI Receptionist, scheduling, payments, and review management.
It offers three tiers — Pro, Elite, and Ultimate — with Pro starting at $199/month and higher tiers requiring a custom quote.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Weave is for
paragraphs: Weave is aimed at dental and medical practices, along with other small businesses, that want to automate patient communication, scheduling, payments, and review management in one platform.

enrichment.short_description: Agentic AI marketing platform helping multi-location brands manage reviews, listings, social media, and search visibility.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Birdeye is an AI-powered platform for multi-location brands to manage online reviews, business listings, social media, and search visibility.

features.title: Reviews AI
features.description: Generates, monitors, and manages customer reviews across 200+ sites.

features.title: Listings AI
features.description: Optimizes business information across 50+ listing platforms.

features.title: Social AI
features.description: Creates and schedules location-relevant social media content.

features.title: Search AI
features.description: Manages brand visibility on AI search tools like ChatGPT and Perplexity, as well as Google.

features.title: Marketing Automation
features.description: Runs personalized email and SMS campaigns by location.

features.title: Insights AI
features.description: Measures reputation, sentiment, and benchmarks performance across locations.

features.title: Integrations
features.description: Connects with 3,000+ apps, including industry-specific PMS, EHR, POS, and DMS platforms.

pros: Serves major multi-location brands like Aspen Dental, Cracker Barrel, and Smile Brands
Manages reviews across 200+ sites and listings across 50+ platforms from one dashboard
Includes AI-driven visibility management for AI search engines like ChatGPT and Perplexity
Integrates with 3,000+ apps, including industry-specific PMS, EHR, POS, and DMS tools
cons: Pricing is custom and requires a location-based quote via a pricing configurator
No public free plan; only a free brand scan/demo is offered
Built for multi-location brands, which may offer more than single-location businesses need

faqs.question: How is Birdeye priced?
faqs.answer: Birdeye uses outcome-based, custom pricing based on the number of locations, available through a pricing configurator or by contacting sales.

faqs.question: Does Birdeye offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: No traditional free plan or trial is mentioned; a free brand scan tool is available instead.

faqs.question: How many review sites does Birdeye monitor?
faqs.answer: Birdeye's Reviews AI covers 200+ review sites.

faqs.question: Does Birdeye help with AI search visibility?
faqs.answer: Yes, its Search AI feature manages brand visibility on AI tools like ChatGPT and Perplexity in addition to Google.

use_cases.title: Multi-location healthcare and dental brands
use_cases.description: Chains can centralize review management, listings, and patient-facing communication across locations.

use_cases.title: Retail and restaurant chains
use_cases.description: Retail and restaurant brands can manage location-specific social content and review responses at scale.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Birdeye?
paragraphs: Birdeye is an agentic AI marketing platform that helps multi-location brands manage their online presence, including reviews, business listings, social media, and visibility on both traditional and AI search engines.
Pricing is customized based on the number of locations and is obtained through a pricing configurator or by contacting sales.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Birdeye is for
paragraphs: Birdeye is built for multi-location brands and enterprises — including healthcare, dental, automotive, restaurant, and retail businesses — that need to manage reputation and visibility across many locations from one platform.

enrichment.short_description: AI-powered reputation management platform for multi-location brands to manage reviews, listings, surveys, and local visibility.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Reputation.com is an AI-powered platform for multi-location brands to manage reviews, listings, and surveys, starting at $80/location/month.

features.title: Rep Score
features.description: A unified performance KPI that benchmarks reputation across locations.

features.title: Reviews management
features.description: Generates and monitors customer reviews across sites.

features.title: Business listings management
features.description: Manages business listing accuracy across the web.

features.title: Reputation IQ
features.description: AI-powered analytics and answers about brand reputation.

features.title: Survey collection and analysis
features.description: Collects customer surveys, with advanced templates on higher-tier plans.

features.title: Social media publishing and listening
features.description: Publishes and monitors social content from the platform.

features.title: Actions routing
features.description: Routes customer feedback and issues to the right team for accountability.

pros: Transparent published pricing starting at $80/location/month
Includes an AI Reputation Manager and Pulse Analytics on the mid-tier plan
Supports English, French, and German
Serves large multi-location clients like Kia UK and Greystar
cons: No free plan or free trial mentioned
Advanced survey and analytics features require higher-priced tiers
Enterprise tier for 125+ locations requires a custom quote

faqs.question: How much does Reputation.com cost?
faqs.answer: Plans start at $80/location/month for Rep Core, with Rep Core + Pulse at $115/location/month and Rep Core + Surveys at $150/location/month.

faqs.question: Does Reputation.com offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: No free plan or trial is mentioned on the pricing page.

faqs.question: What languages does Reputation.com support?
faqs.answer: The platform is documented in English, French, and German.

faqs.question: What is included in the Enterprise plan?
faqs.answer: The Enterprise plan, for 125+ locations, includes custom pricing, dedicated infrastructure, and a customer success manager.

use_cases.title: Automotive dealership groups
use_cases.description: Dealership groups can track review sentiment and listing accuracy across multiple locations.

use_cases.title: Healthcare and senior living networks
use_cases.description: Multi-site healthcare and senior living organizations can monitor reputation and run patient/resident surveys.
use_cases.audience: Healthcare systems and senior living communities

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Reputation?
paragraphs: Reputation (reputation.com) is an AI-powered reputation management platform that helps multi-location brands manage reviews, business listings, surveys, and social media from one dashboard, scored through its Rep Score KPI.
Pricing is published per location, starting at $80/location/month and rising with added survey and analytics capabilities.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Reputation is for
paragraphs: Reputation is built for multi-location businesses — including automotive dealerships, healthcare systems, senior living communities, retail chains, and financial institutions — that need to manage online reputation at scale.

enrichment.short_description: Reputation management software for monitoring, analyzing, and responding to customer reviews across multiple platforms.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: ReviewTrackers helps multi-location businesses monitor, analyze, and respond to customer reviews and local listings across platforms.

features.title: Review monitoring and management
features.description: Tracks and manages customer reviews across multiple platforms.

features.title: Local SEO optimization
features.description: Manages local listings to support search visibility.

features.title: Customer experience analytics
features.description: Analyzes review data for customer experience insights.

features.title: Competitor analysis
features.description: Benchmarks review performance against competitors.

features.title: Unified social and review monitoring
features.description: Combines social media and review monitoring in a single workflow.

features.title: Employer brand monitoring
features.description: Tracks employer review sites alongside customer reviews.

features.title: Review widgets
features.description: Displays customer reviews on business websites.

pros: Trusted by well-known brands like OneMain Financial, Carvana, and Ashley Furniture
Available on web, iOS, and Android
Combines review, social, and local listing monitoring in one workflow
Offices across the US, Australia, New Zealand, and Singapore support customers in multiple regions
cons: Pricing is not published and requires a demo request
No free plan or free trial mentioned
AI capabilities are not detailed on the website

faqs.question: How much does ReviewTrackers cost?
faqs.answer: Pricing is tiered but not disclosed publicly; prospective customers must request a demo.

faqs.question: Does ReviewTrackers have a mobile app?
faqs.answer: Yes, it offers apps for both iOS and Android.

faqs.question: Where is ReviewTrackers based?
faqs.answer: ReviewTrackers lists offices in Chicago, Salt Lake City, Sydney, Auckland, and Singapore.

faqs.question: What kinds of businesses use ReviewTrackers?
faqs.answer: It serves multi-location businesses across industries including retail, financial services, dental, furniture, and storage.

use_cases.title: Multi-location retail and financial brands
use_cases.description: Businesses with many locations can monitor and respond to reviews at scale while tracking local SEO performance.
use_cases.audience: Multi-location retail and financial services businesses

use_cases.title: Dental and furniture businesses
use_cases.description: Service businesses can combine review collection, social monitoring, and competitor analysis in one workflow.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is ReviewTrackers?
paragraphs: ReviewTrackers is online reputation management software that helps businesses monitor, analyze, and respond to customer reviews across multiple platforms, alongside local listing and social media monitoring.
It offers tiered subscription pricing that is not publicly disclosed, with mobile apps available for iOS and Android.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who ReviewTrackers is for
paragraphs: ReviewTrackers is designed for multi-location businesses and enterprises across industries like retail, financial services, dental, furniture, and storage that need to manage customer feedback at scale.
